key_insight: Machine learning models showed strong overall performance for predicting osteoporosis risk, especially deep learning and imaging-based approaches.
clinical_relevance: These models may help identify adults at higher risk of osteoporosis or low bone mineral density earlier, supporting targeted screening and prevention efforts.
method: Systematic review of studies published from Jan 2020 to Feb 2026
Meta-analysis pooled AUCs using random-effects models with logit transformation
Subgroups compared data type, model class, external validation, and population type
evidence_signal: Across 27 studies in meta-analysis, pooled AUC was 0.879, with imaging models, deep learning, and internally validated models performing best; heterogeneity was very high (I2 = 99.5%).
watch_item: Results should be interpreted cautiously because most studies had limited external validation, substantial heterogeneity, and publication bias was detected.
paper_title: Machine Learning Models for Osteoporosis Prediction: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
